articles.title: REACT QUERY SELECTORS, SUPERCHARGED
articles.summary: The `select` option in React Query allows for fine-grained subscriptions to specific parts of data fetched from an API. Using `select` prevents unnecessary re-renders by subscribing components only to the data they need, improving performance. For computationally expensive `select` functions, `useCallback` should be used for stable references and libraries like `fast-memoize` for memoization.

articles.title: HOW WE REPLACED ELASTICSEARCH AND MONGODB WITH RUST AND ROCKSDB
articles.summary: Radar, a geolocation infrastructure company, replaced its Elasticsearch and MongoDB setup with HorizonDB, a new geospatial database written in Rust. This change was due to the high costs and operational complexities of the previous system. HorizonDB was built using technologies like RocksDB and S2, and it improved performance, scalability, and developer experience.

articles.title: NEXT.JS MIGRATION - CHANGELOG
articles.summary: The LLM Gateway team migrated from TanStack Start (alpha version) to Next.js and saw performance improvements, including perfect Lighthouse scores for Performance and SEO (both jumping to 100), and a Speed Index improvement from 2.4s to 1.1s. The switch was motivated by Next.js' better built-in optimizations, simpler development model, edge compatibility, and better system resource efficiency with more stable CPU and memory usage.

articles.title: CLAUDE IS THE DRUG, CURSOR IS THE DEALER
articles.summary: Many AI startups are essentially distributors ("dealers") for the models created by larger AI labs ("chemists") like OpenAI and Anthropic. These startups, despite high valuations and user growth, don't control the core product or supply chain, making them vulnerable. There are two possible futures: either the labs will go direct, cutting out the wrappers, or models will become commoditized, allowing the wrappers to thrive.

articles.title: RUST, PYTHON, AND TYPESCRIPT: THE NEW TRIFECTA
articles.summary: Rust, Python, and TypeScript will become the dominant programming languages as AI-powered coding tools shift language selection from tribal loyalty to practical fundamentals. This new "idea-oriented programming" paradigm has the programmer as chief architect while AI handles implementation, making strong type systems, healthy ecosystems, and good package managers more important than syntax preferences. These three languages perform well in their respective domains (Rust for performance, Python for ML/data science, and TypeScript for web).

articles.title: THE VALUE OF INSTITUTIONAL MEMORY
articles.summary: Institutional memory is important, and there are often negative consequences that come up when organizations forget crucial information or processes. For example, in history, the Chesterfield Canal disaster, Volkswagen's emissions scandals, and the Space Shuttle explosions show how a lack of institutional memory can lead to costly mistakes and tragedies. Forgetting can occur due to employee turnover, technological obsolescence, deliberate deletion, or simply a decline in activity leading to skill atrophy.

articles.title: V0.DEV -> V0.APP
articles.summary: v0.dev is now v0.app, an AI-powered platform that allows anyone to create and deploy fully functional applications with a single prompt. This new version uses agentic AI, enabling it to research, reason, debug, plan, and handle complex app development tasks autonomously.

articles.title: PANDA
articles.summary: Panda is a universal and type-safe CSS-in-JS framework for web dev. It allows writing styles using style objects or props that are extracted at build time, generating modern CSS with features like cascade layers and CSS variables. Panda supports various JavaScript frameworks and has recipes, variants, design tokens, and type-safe styling.

articles.title: FORESIGHTJS
articles.summary: ForesightJS is a lightweight JavaScript library designed to predict user intent and prefetch content for improved perceived speed on both desktop and mobile devices. It uses strategies like mouse trajectory prediction, keyboard navigation tracking, and viewport enter detection.

articles.title: GITHUB JUST GOT LESS INDEPENDENT AT MICROSOFT AFTER CEO RESIGNATION
articles.summary: GitHub CEO Thomas Dohmke has resigned to become a startup founder. The CEO position will not be replaced. GitHub's leadership team will report directly to Microsoft's CoreAI team.

articles.title: FUTURE AI BILLS OF $100K/YR PER DEV
articles.summary: AI coding tools are rising in cost due to constant token prices and increased token consumption. The industry's initial assumption of decreasing costs has been wrong, leading to throttling on paid plans like Cursor's. Kilo, along with other open-source tools like Cline and Roo, try to mitigate these costs by offering users more control and cost-saving features.

articles.title: LINEAR SENT ME DOWN A LOCAL-FIRST RABBIT HOLE
articles.summary: Linear, a project management tool, is fast because it's local-first. There are various production-ready local-first solutions for building something like this, like Electric SQL, PowerSync, and Jazz. Despite the trade-offs of blind servers, permanent data storage, and local development quirks, the author found Jazz to be impressive and productive, and plans to explore Electric SQL and Zero more.

articles.title: CLAUDE CODE IS ALL YOU NEED
articles.summary: Claude Code can be used in various projects ranging from autonomous startup demos to practical tasks like bank statement organization, and is surprisingly good as a text editor.

articles.title: UI VS. API. VS. UAI
articles.summary: The evolution of application interfaces has progressed from UI (human-operated) to API (application-operated) to UAI (agent-operated).

articles.title: GO FEATURES BY VERSION
articles.summary: A summary of the major features introduced in each version of the Go programming language, from version 1.0 to the upcoming version 1.25.

articles.title: I TRIED EVERY TODO APP AND ENDED UP WITH A .TXT FILE
articles.summary: The most effective system for this dev ended up being a simple `.txt` file for daily task management.
